name: Mexican Bean Dip
directions: Mix taco seasoning with beans and spread in a 13x9 inch glass dish.
Layer sour cream, onion, peppers, lettuce, tomatoes, cheese& olives in that order.
Serve with tortilla chips or scooper size Fritos.

name: Bean Dip with a kick
directions: Combine soup, cheese, and salsa in a saucepan over medium heat.
Heat until cheese is melted and it's heated through.
Make sure you stir it often.
Serve with tortilla chips or your favorite dippers.

name: Layered Taco Dip
directions: Mix mayonnaise, sour cream and taco seasoning and set aside.
On a large platter or 9 X 13 casserole dish spread refried beans as the first layer of the dip.
Next spread the guacamole.
Then cover with the sour cream and mayonnaise mixture.
Sprinkle with the cheddar cheese.
Top with black olives, tomatoes and green onions.
Serve with Tostados.

name: Fiesta Ranch Bean Dip
directions: Combine ingredients except cheese.
Heat through.
Top with cheese and serve with tortilla chips.

name: Tom's Layered Mexican Dip
directions: Beat avacados with lemon juice, picante sauce, grated onion& 1/2 c.
sour cream until smooth--guacamole.
(I use this for my guacamole with other recipes too, I love it so much.) Using 2 large platters or 2 9x13 in.
pans, place half the bean dip into bottom of each.
Carefully smooth half the guacamole on top of bean layer, and 1 c.
of sour cream on top of guac.
layer.
Next layer half of the tomatoes, half the scallions, half the olives, and half the grated cheese into each pan.
Chill and serve with chips.

name: Baja Dip
directions: Spread the hummus over the bottom of a 1-quart glass dish.
Next, sprinkle evenly with avocado.
Sprinkle lime juice over the avocado; season lightly with salt and pepper if desired.
Top with salsa, sour cream, and cilantro.
Cover and chill until ready to serve.
Serve with tortilla chips.

name: Refried Bean Dip
directions: Mix together refried beans and 1/4 of salsa.
Place mixture in bottom of 8 x 8 glass dish.
Pour remaining salsa on top of bean mixture.
sprinkle half of taco seasoning or to taste over salsa.
Sprinkle chopped onions over salsa mixture.
Spread sour cream over salsa mixture.
Sprinkle cheese over top of sour cream.
Cover and chill over night.
Serve with Corn chips, crackers, dipping utensiles ect--.

name: Mexican Layered Bean Dip
directions: In a 13x9 inch baking pan, layer:.
refried beans.
sour cream and mayo mixed, then spread.
salsa.
cheese.
olives.
and bake 20 minutes at 400 or microwave 10 minutes.
In the meantime, heat up some oil in a saute pan and start frying the chips.  Once cooked, lay them on a papertowel to drain and salt immediately.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Green Chile and Corn Layered Dip

name: Layered Mexican Dip
directions: Layer in the bottom of pan (I use the disposable foil 13x9 inches pan, have also used 2 disposable pie plates.) the can of refried beans.
Make guacamole by mashing with a fork or in food processor the 2 avacados with the salsa, onion, lemon juice and 1/4 Cup sour cream. Place guacamole layer on top of beans.
Spread rest of sour cream over guacamole.
Sprinkle cheese layer over sour cream layer.
Sprinkle tomatoes and then scallions over cheese. (Olives too, if you like em).
Cover and chill before serving.
Serve with tortilla chips or veggies, soooo good.

name: Beth's Taco Dip
directions: Spread beans evenly on the bottom of a baking dish or casserole. (I use a quiche dish).
Mash avocados with chili powder, onion, pepper and garlic. Spread avocado mixture over the beans. Next, layer the sour cream over the first two layers.
Mix together the taco sauce, cheese, jalapeño sauce, olives, scallions, and tomatoes. Spread over the sour cream layer.
Serve with tortilla chips.

name: Bean Burrito Dip
directions: Mix refried beans, pinto beans, Rotel tomato's, cheese (I leave a little cheese to add to the top before I bake) and place in an 8x8 baking dish.
Bake at 350 for 20-30 minutes ot until heated thru.

After cooling slightly, put a layer of sour cream (depending on taste, I like a little thicker layer myself) followed by the chopped onion and if you choose, the jalapeno's (I put the jalapeno's on top so people can eat around them if they want).
Serve with dip size Fritos or Tortilla chips.
(I have used this same recipe with flour tortillas for simple bean burritos at a childs birthday party. I just used mild Rotel and nixed the jalapeno's).

name: Mom's Mexican Bean Dip
directions: Preheat oven to 275 degrees.
Spread refried beans out on the bottom of a 8 x 8" baking dish.
Mix the sour cream with the package of taco seasoning.
Spread the seasoned sour cream over top of the beans.
Spread the cheese over the sour cream.
Heat in the oven until the cheese melts.
Just before serving spread the vegetables over top of the melted cheese.
Serve with your favorite tortilla chips, my favorite with this dish is the hint of lime chips.

name: Layered Nacho Dip
directions: Mix layer 1 and spread evenly on a 14-16 inch platter with slight sides.
Mix layer 2 and spread to within an inch of layer 1.
Mix layer 3 and repeat.
Mix the cheeses and repeat.
Beat the layer 5 ingredients with an electric mixer until smooth and spread over cheese.
Sprinkle tomato and green onion all over and serve your masterpiece!

similar.name: Green Chile and Corn Layered Dip

name: Refried Beans
directions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees.  Spray a 2-quart casserole or 9-inch baking dish with cooking spray.
Combine all ingredients except sour cream and cilantro.  Spoon into casserole; cover.
Bake for 30 minutes or until hot.  Top with sour cream and cilantro before serving, if desired.

name: Green Chile and Corn Layered Dip
directions: In mixing bowl, combine cream cheese, mayonnaise, chili powder and cumin. Beat till combined, set aside. Combine drained corn and green chilies.   In 8 x 8" pan, spread refried beans evenly over the bottom.  Layer cream cheese mixture over beans then top with the corn and chilies, onion and cheese.  Cover tightly and refrigerate till serving time.  Serve with tortilla chips.
